flashcards.back: A cyclic group is a group that can be generated by a single element. This means there exists an element g in the group such that every element of the group can be expressed as g^n for some integer n.

flashcards.front: Define the Order of a Group
flashcards.back: The order of a group G, denoted |G|, is the number of elements in the group. If the group has finite number of elements, it is a finite group; otherwise, it is an infinite group.

flashcards.front: Define the Order of an Element
flashcards.back: The order of an element a in a group G is the smallest positive integer n such that a^n = e (identity element). If no such n exists, the element has infinite order.
The Klein four-group V4 = {e, a, b, ab} is an abelian group where each non-identity element has order 2. It is isomorphic to Z2 × Z2 and represents the symmetries of a rectangle.

flashcards.front: What is the Symmetric Group Sn?
flashcards.back: The symmetric group Sn consists of all permutations of n elements under composition. It has n! elements and is non-abelian for n ≥ 3.

flashcards.front: What is the Alternating Group An?
flashcards.back: The alternating group An is the set of all even permutations of n elements. It is a subgroup of Sn with n!/2 elements and is abelian only for n ≤ 3.

flashcards.front: What is the Dihedral Group Dn?
flashcards.back: The dihedral group Dn represents symmetries of a regular n-gon, containing n rotations and n reflections. It has 2n elements and is non-abelian for n ≥ 3.

flashcards.front: What is Zn under addition modulo n?
flashcards.back: Zn = {0, 1, 2, ..., n-1} with operation addition modulo n forms a cyclic group of order n. It is generated by element 1.

flashcards.front: What is the Unit Group U(n)?
flashcards.back: U(n) = {a ∈ {1,2,...,n-1} : gcd(a,n) = 1} under multiplication modulo n. The order of U(n) is φ(n) (Euler's totient function).
A trivial group contains only the identity element. It is denoted as {e} or simply e, and any group with one element is trivially cyclic.

flashcards.front: What is a Generator of a Cyclic Group?
flashcards.back: A generator is an element g such that repeatedly applying the group operation to g produces all elements of the group. A cyclic group may have multiple generators.

flashcards.front: What is the Quaternion Group Q8?
flashcards.back: Q8 = {1, -1, i, -i, j, -j, k, -k} with specific multiplication rules. It has order 8, is non-abelian, and every element except -1 has order 4.
Yes, every cyclic group is abelian. If G = ⟨g⟩, then for any elements g^a, g^b in G, g^a · g^b = g^(a+b) = g^b · g^a.
Given an element a in group G, the cyclic subgroup generated by a is ⟨a⟩ = {a^n : n ∈ Z}. It is the smallest subgroup containing a.

flashcards.front: How many generators does Zp have when p is prime?
flashcards.back: When p is prime, Zp has φ(p) = p-1 generators. All non-zero elements are generators since every element has order p.
